text:
Gustave watched the leaves swirl as they danced in the breeze. They were colorful, just like his dreams. Tonight was the town’s annual foliage festival, and Gustave was looking forward to the autumn leaves. He’d collect them, let them decorate his home.

Tonight wasn’t just about leaves; it was his birthday. He imagined himself back home, nestled among the giant oak piles he’d always visited.

He climbed into his car and drove far into the woods. He’d found these places secret where old oaks grew. They were home.

His house was a simple shack, charming with aged wood, but full. Gustave loved his garden. Leaves made patterns on the soil, shapes like landscapes. Tonight, he’d make a tiny garden city out of all the autumn leaves he could find.

The path took him to a clearing. There, among the rows of autumn-colored ornaments, was his favorite bird. A bluejay perched on a sprig of holly.
